openmeter/watermill/driver/kafka.purpose: Sarama/Watermill Kafka driver providing Publisher and Subscriber factories with OTel tracing, SASL/SSL auth, partition-key routing, and go-metrics-to-OTel bridging. All callers get a ready-to-use watermill Publisher or message.Subscriber; no Sarama internals leak out.

patterns.name: BrokerOptions as shared config root
patterns.description: Both PublisherOptions and SubscriberOptions embed BrokerOptions. Call BrokerOptions.Validate() then createKafkaConfig(role) to obtain a *sarama.Config — never build sarama.Config manually.

patterns.name: Role suffix on ClientID
patterns.description: createKafkaConfig appends the role string ('publisher' or 'subscriber') to ClientID via fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", clientID, role). Always pass a non-empty role string.

patterns.name: OTel tracing via kafka.NewOTELSaramaTracer()
patterns.description: Publisher must set wmConfig.Tracer = kafka.NewOTELSaramaTracer() — it relies on the global trace provider set up by app/common/telemetry.go. Subscriber has no tracer field; tracing is handled at the router level.

patterns.name: Partition-key from metadata via marshalerWithPartitionKey
patterns.description: Set msg.Metadata[PartitionKeyMetadataKey] before publishing to control Kafka partition. The custom marshaler reads this key, sets kafkaMsg.Key, and removes the header so it doesn't appear in the Kafka record.

patterns.name: SaramaMetricRenamer drops noisy metrics
patterns.description: Pass SaramaMetricRenamer(role) as NameTransformFn when creating the metrics.Registry. It prefixes names with 'sarama.', adds a 'role' attribute, and drops per-broker/per-topic and compression metrics to avoid cardinality explosion.

patterns.name: Topic provisioning before publisher creation
patterns.description: NewPublisher calls TopicProvisioner.Provision(ctx, topics...) before kafka.NewPublisher. New publishers must pass ProvisionTopics and a TopicProvisioner; missing either silently skips provisioning.
patterns.example: if err = in.TopicProvisioner.Provision(ctx, in.ProvisionTopics...); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Validate() before any construction
patterns.description: PublisherOptions.Validate() and SubscriberOptions.Validate() must be called at the top of each factory function. Constructors return early on validation errors — do not skip.
patterns.example: if err := in.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

key_file_guides.role: Defines BrokerOptions and createKafkaConfig — the single place where Sarama config is built, SASL/TLS is applied, and the OTel metrics registry is wired.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MetricMeter is required; createKafkaConfig will error if nil. ApiVersionsRequest is explicitly disabled to suppress noisy Sarama logs — do not re-enable.

key_file_guides.role: Factory for kafka.Publisher: validates options, builds Sarama config, provisions topics, sets OTel tracer, and uses marshalerWithPartitionKey.
key_file_guides.watch_for: marshalerWithPartitionKey{} is hardcoded — do not replace with kafka.DefaultMarshaler or partition key routing breaks.

key_file_guides.role: Factory for message.Subscriber: validates options, builds Sarama config, sets defaultMaxProcessingTime (5m), and creates a Watermill Kafka subscriber.
key_file_guides.watch_for: defaultMaxProcessingTime is 5 minutes — consumers that do heavy processing may lose partition assignment if they exceed this; it is intentionally high but not infinite.

key_file_guides.role: marshalerWithPartitionKey wraps DefaultMarshaler to promote the x-kafka-partition-key metadata field to the Sarama message Key. AddPartitionKeyFromSubject is the helper callers use.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The metadata key is stripped from headers after extraction — callers must not rely on the header arriving at consumers.

key_file_guides.role: SaramaMetricRenamer returns a TransformMetricsNameToOtel function that filters and renames go-metrics names for OTel. Drop logic is inline — add new drop prefixes here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: per-broker ('for-broker') and per-topic ('for-topic') metrics are dropped to avoid label cardinality explosion. Do not remove these guards.

key_file_guides.role: SaramaLoggerAdaptor adapts slog.Logger to sarama.StdLogger interface. Both sarama.Logger and sarama.DebugLogger are set in createKafkaConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: sarama.Logger and sarama.DebugLogger are globals — setting them here affects all Sarama instances in the process.

key_file_guides.role: XDGSCRAMClient implements sarama.SCRAMClient for SCRAM-SHA-256 and SCRAM-SHA-512. Required for SASL_SSL with SCRAM mechanisms.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This is a verbatim port of the official Sarama SCRAM example — do not modify the cryptographic logic.

anti_patterns: Building *sarama.Config directly instead of using createKafkaConfig — SASL/TLS, metric registry, and ClientID suffix are all missing.
Using kafka.DefaultMarshaler{} in PublisherConfig instead of marshalerWithPartitionKey{} — partition key routing silently stops working.
Skipping TopicProvisioner.Provision before publishing — topics may not exist in the Kafka cluster, causing publish errors at runtime.
Setting a nil MetricMeter in BrokerOptions — metrics.NewRegistry returns an error and construction fails.
Adding per-broker or per-topic metric labels without a Drop rule in SaramaMetricRenamer — creates unbounded OTel label cardinality.

decisions.decision: marshalerWithPartitionKey wraps DefaultMarshaler rather than replacing it
decisions.rationale: Preserves all existing header/UUID behaviour from DefaultMarshaler while adding partition key promotion in a single override point.

decisions.decision: OTel metrics registry injected into sarama.Config.MetricRegistry
decisions.rationale: Sarama uses go-metrics internally; the metrics sub-package bridges those calls to OTel without forking Sarama or using reflection beyond factory unwrapping.

decisions.decision: Topic provisioning is a Publisher-side responsibility
decisions.rationale: Publishers know the full topic list they need; centralising provisioning here avoids requiring callers (app/common wiring) to sequence topic creation separately.

code_examples.scenario: Create a Publisher with OTel metrics, SASL/TLS, partition key support, and topic provisioning
